   Sets: 2 | Reps: 15 | Notes: Reduced from 3 to 2 sets. Set 1: right side. Set 2: left side. (~2.5 min)

4. Banded Terminal Knee Extensions
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 12 | Notes: Reduced from 3×15 to 2×12. Set 1: right leg. Set 2: left leg. (~2.5 min)

5. ATG Split Squat
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 6 each leg | Notes: Reduced from 3×8 to 2×6. Set 1: right leg forward, 6 reps. Set 2: left leg forward, 6 reps. Set 3: right leg, 6 reps. Set 4: left leg, 6 reps. Actually — 4 total sets (2 per leg). (~5 min)

6. Cossack Squat
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 12 | Notes: Alternating exercise — doubled from 6 to 12 reps per set (6 each side). Reduced from 3 to 2 sets. (~3 min)

7. Active Frog Stretch
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 30s | Notes: Reduced from 45s to 30s. Bilateral, no side issue. (~2 min)
```

**Estimated total: ~21 minutes**

**What moved out:** Tibialis Anterior Raises moved to Session C (ankle/Achilles is the natural home — it was misplaced in the knee/hip session). Copenhagen Plank moved to Session D (it's a core exercise at heart).

**Note on ATG Split Squats:** This is the one exercise I'm giving 4 sets to (2 per leg) because it's the single most important gate-test exercise in this session — it builds toward Cossack squat depth, pistol squat prerequisites, and knee resilience. The extra volume is worth it.

---

## ROUTINE: Session C — Ankle/Achilles & Integration
Target time: 23 minutes

```
1. Extended Ankle CARs
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 5 each direction | Notes: Reduced from 2×8 to 2×5. Set 1: right foot (5 CW, 5 CCW = 10 reps). Set 2: left foot (5 CW, 5 CCW = 10 reps). Total: 4 sets in Hevy (2 per foot). (~4 min)

2. Tibialis Anterior Raises
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 15 | Notes: MOVED FROM SESSION B. Bilateral. Reduced from 3 to 2 sets. Critical for Achilles rehab — belongs with ankle work. (~2 min)

3. Eccentric Calf Raises
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 12 | Notes: Reduced from 3×15 to 2×12. Set 1: right leg. Set 2: left leg. Set 3: right leg. Set 4: left leg. 4 total sets in Hevy (2 per leg). Slow 3-second eccentrics. (~5 min)

4. Wall Dorsiflexion Mobilization
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 25s | Notes: Reduced from 3×30s to 2×25s. Set 1: right side. Set 2: left side. 2 total sets in Hevy, timer works per side. (~2 min)

5. Single-Leg Balance (Eyes Closed)
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 25s | Notes: Reduced from 3×30s to 2×25s. Set 1: right leg. Set 2: left leg. Set 3: right leg. Set 4: left leg. 4 total sets in Hevy (2 per leg). Gate test is 30s — we'll progress to that. (~4.5 min)

6. Supported Deep Squat Hold
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 30s | Notes: Reduced from 3×30-45s to 2×30s. Bilateral. (~2 min)

7. Bear Crawl
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 20s | Notes: Reduced from 2×30s to 2×20s. Bilateral. (~1.5 min)
```

**Estimated total: ~21 minutes**

**What moved out:** Dead Bugs moved to Session D (it's a core exercise). Tibialis Anterior Raises moved IN from Session B.

---

## ROUTINE: Session D — Core Stability
Target time: 24 minutes

```
1. Dead Bugs
   Sets: 3 | Reps: 16 | Notes: Alternating exercise — doubled from 8 to 16 reps per set (8 each side). Consolidated from Sessions C and D. 3 sets here covers total volume. (~4 min)

2. Open Book
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 8 | Notes: MOVED FROM SESSION A. Set 1: 8 reps rotating right. Set 2: 8 reps rotating left. Rotational work pairs well with core day. (~2.5 min)

3. Pallof Press
   Sets: 2 | Reps: 8 | Notes: Reduced from 3×10 to 2×8. Set 1: right side. Set 2: left side. Anti-rotation focus. (~2.5 min)

4. Bird Dogs
   Sets: 3 | Reps: 16 | Notes: Alternating exercise — doubled from 8 to 16 reps per set (8 each side). (~4 min)

5. Hollow Body Hold
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 25s | Notes: Reduced from 3×30s to 2×25s. Bilateral. Combined with Session A's hollow body — you're now doing 2 sets in A and 2 sets in D per week, which matches the original 3-set volume spread across two sessions. Gate test is 30s. (~2 min)

6. Copenhagen Plank
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 20s | Notes: MOVED FROM SESSION B. Set 1: right side (top leg). Set 2: left side. Set 3: right side. Set 4: left side. 4 total sets in Hevy (2 per side). Reduced from 3×20-30s to 2×20s per side. (~4.5 min)

7. Suitcase Carry
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 25s | Notes: Reduced from 3×30s to 2×25s. Set 1: right hand. Set 2: left hand. (~2.5 min)
```

**Estimated total: ~22 minutes**

---

## ROUTINE: Active Recovery
Target time: 25 minutes (unchanged concept, just accurate timing)

```
1. Stationary Bike
   Sets: 1 | Duration: 15 min | Notes: Unchanged. Easy pace, zone 1-2. (~15 min)

2. Foam Roll Quads & IT Band
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 90s | Notes: Set 1: right leg 90s. Set 2: left leg 90s. Reduced from 2min to 90s per side. (~4 min)

3. Foam Roll Calves
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 90s | Notes: Set 1: right leg 90s. Set 2: left leg 90s. Reduced from 2min to 90s per side. (~4 min)

4. Lacrosse Ball Shoulder
   Sets: 2 | Duration: 90s | Notes: Set 1: right shoulder 90s. Set 2: left shoulder 90s. Reduced from 2min to 90s per side. (~4 min)
```

**Estimated total: ~27 minutes** (active recovery can run longer — it's low intensity)

---

## Summary of Changes

### What Was Cut/Reduced and Why

| Change | Rationale |
|--------|-----------|
| CARs reps: 5→3 per direction | 3 slow, intentional reps explore full ROM just as well. Quality over quantity for joint maintenance. |
| Dead Hangs: 3→2 sets | 2×20-30s is plenty for passive shoulder decompression in Phase 1. Volume increases in Build/Load. |
| Most exercises: 3→2 sets | Phase 1 Foundation (weeks 1-2) is about motor patterning and tissue tolerance, not volume. 2 sets provides the stimulus. |
| Duration reductions (30s→25s, 45s→30s) | Small trims that add up. Still provides meaningful time under tension. Gate test targets are maintained via progression. |

### What Was Redistributed

| Exercise | From | To | Why |
|----------|------|----|-----|
| Open Book | Session A | Session D | Rotational work pairs with core day; freed space in overloaded Session A |
| Tibialis Anterior Raises | Session B | Session C | Ankle/Achilles session is the natural home for tibialis work |
| Copenhagen Plank | Session B | Session D | It's a core exercise — consolidating core work on core day |
| Dead Bugs | Sessions C + D | Session D only | Consolidated into one session with higher per-set reps (alternating = doubled) |

### Gate Test Readiness Assessment

| Gate Test | Status | Coverage |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Pain-free planks | ON TRACK | Hollow body (A+D), Copenhagen plank (D), scap push-ups (A) |
| 8 strict pull-ups | ON TRACK | Dead hangs (A) build grip/shoulder endurance; pull-up volume begins Phase 2 |
| 15 clean dips | ON TRACK | Scap push-ups (A), wall slides (A) build prerequisite shoulder stability |
| Full Cossack squat | ON TRACK | Cossack squats (B), 90/90 switches (B), ATG split squats (B) |
| 30s single-leg balance | ON TRACK | Single-leg balance (C) starts at 25s, progresses to 30s+ |
| 4in wall dorsiflexion | ON TRACK | Wall dorsiflexion (C), extended ankle CARs (C), eccentric calf raises (C) |
| 30s hollow body hold | ON TRACK | Hollow body in both A and D; starts at 20-25s, progresses to 30s+ |
| Overhead wall test | ON TRACK | Wall slides (A), T-spine extension (A), shoulder CARs (daily) |

**No concerns.** Every gate test exercise is preserved. The volume reductions are appropriate for Foundation weeks and will be restored through progression.

### Progression Recommendations

**Build Phase (Weeks 3-4):**
- CARs: Keep at 3 reps but add 2-second end-range holds (PAILs/RAILs lite)
- Add 1 set to: Dead Hangs (→3), ATG Split Squats (→3 per leg = 6 total sets), Hollow Body Hold (→3 in Session D)
- Increase duration: Single-leg balance →30s, Copenhagen plank →25s, Wall dorsiflexion →30s
- Increase reps: Banded external rotation →12, Eccentric calf raises →15

**Load Phase (Weeks 5-8):**
- Add external load to: ATG Split Squats (light DB), Cossack Squats (light DB), Glute Bridges (→single-leg variant)
- Progress: Dead Hangs →active hangs with scap engagement, Scap Push-ups →push-up negatives
- Duration targets at gate-test levels: 30s hollow body, 30s single-leg balance, 30s Copenhagen plank
- Add 1 set across the board to key exercises (back to 3 working sets)
- Begin testing gate criteria at week 7 to identify weak points for targeted work in week 8

The fitness coach analysis is back. Here's the restructured plan:

## Key Changes

**Volume strategy:** Drop most exercises from 3→2 sets for Foundation (weeks 1-2), then progressively restore volume in Build/Load phases. CARs reduced from 5→3 reps per direction (quality over quantity).

**Redistributed exercises:**
- **Open Book** → Session A to Session D (rotational work pairs with core)
- **Tibialis Raises** → Session B to Session C (belongs with ankle/Achilles)
- **Copenhagen Plank** → Session B to Session D (consolidate core work)
- **Dead Bugs** → consolidated into Session D only (with doubled reps for alternating)

**Bilateral handling:**
- Alternating exercises (dead bugs, bird dogs, hip switches, Cossack squats): double the reps per set
- Sequential unilateral (clamshells, calf raises, balance, Pallof press): separate set per side
- CARs "each direction": reps doubled within set; "each limb": separate set per limb

**Estimated times:** Daily CARs ~11 min, Sessions A-D: 21-24 min each

**All gate tests remain covered.** Progression plan restores 3rd sets and gate-test durations during Build (weeks 3-4) and Load (weeks 5-8).

input.prompt:
You are an expert fitness coach. You previously restructured Ryan's routines, but the time constraint was misunderstood. Here's the correction:

## CORRECTED CONSTRAINT

Ryan's total workout window is **25-30 minutes, including Daily CARs**. Every session day includes CARs as a warmup, so:
- Daily CARs: ~10 min
- Main session (A, B, C, or D): **15-18 minutes max**
- Total: 25-28 minutes

**This time budget is PERMANENT** — it does not expand in Build or Load phases. Do not plan to "progressively restore volume" — the volume must fit in 15-18 minutes per session at all phases. Load phase adds intensity (weight, depth, hold times), not more sets.

## WHAT STAYS FROM THE PREVIOUS RESTRUCTURE

Keep all the bilateral handling rules:
- Alternating exercises: double reps per set (dead bugs 8→16, bird dogs 8→16, hip switches 8→16, Cossack squats 6→12)
- Sequential unilateral: separate set per side (clamshells, calf raises, balance, external rotation, etc.)
- CARs: reps per direction within set; separate set per limb
- Exercise redistributions (Open Book→D, Tibialis→C, Copenhagen Plank→D, Dead Bugs→D only)

## WHAT NEEDS TO CHANGE

With only 15-18 minutes for the main session, most sessions will need 5-6 exercises max (not 7-8). You need to make harder cuts.

**Options to consider:**
1. Remove exercises that are lower-priority for gate tests
2. Reduce to 1 set for some exercises (1 set of a bilateral exercise = 2 sets in Hevy counting both sides)
3. Combine sessions differently — maybe 3 sessions instead of 4, rotating exercises
4. Move some exercises to "add-on" status for days Ryan has extra time

## CONTEXT REMINDERS

- Ryan is 46, Achilles repair, shoulder instability, desk worker
- Phase 1 gate tests: pain-free planks, 8 strict pull-ups, 15 clean dips, full Cossack squat, 30s single-leg balance, 4in wall dorsiflexion, 30s hollow body hold, overhead wall test
- Long-term goals (flexible timeline): muscle-ups, tuck planche, pistol squats, handstands
- All exercises must still appear SOMEWHERE in the weekly plan (but not necessarily every session)

## Daily CARs (from previous restructure, keep as-is at ~10 min)

1. Neck CARs — 1 set × 3 each direction (6 total)
2. Shoulder CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per arm, 6 reps each = 12 total)
3. Hip CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per leg, 6 reps each = 12 total)
4. Ankle CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per foot, 6 reps each = 12 total)
5. Spine CARs — 1 set × 5
6. Wrist CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per wrist, 6 reps each = 12 total)
7. T-Spine Rotation — 2 sets × 5 (set per side)

## OUTPUT FORMAT

For each routine, provide:

```
ROUTINE: [name]
Target time: [X] minutes (must be ≤18 min)

1. Exercise Name
   Hevy sets: [N] | Per-set: Reps [X] or Duration [Xs]

2. ...
```

After routines, provide:
- A brief "Add-on" list: exercises that got cut from main sessions but should be done when Ryan has extra time
- How intensity progresses in Build and Load phases WITHOUT adding time
- Gate test coverage check

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the current program. Let me analyze the exercise inventory and time budgets carefully before designing the restructured routines.

Here is the full analysis and restructured plan:

---

## TIME BUDGET ANALYSIS

Each main session must fit in **15-18 minutes**. That means roughly:
- 5 exercises at ~3 min each = 15 min
- 6 exercises at ~2.5 min each = 15 min
- 6 exercises at ~3 min each = 18 min

So the hard ceiling is **6 exercises per session**, and some need to be quick (1-2 sets).

## EXERCISE INVENTORY (non-CARs, non-recovery)

From the current program, the main session exercises are:

**Shoulder:** Dead Hangs, T-Spine Extension (Foam Roller), Open Book, Scapular Push-ups, Banded External Rotation, Prone Y-T-W Raises, Wall Slides, Shoulder Sleeper Stretch

**Knee/Hip:** 90/90 Hip Switches, Glute Bridge, Banded Clamshells, Tibialis Raises, Banded TKE, ATG Split Squat, Cossack Squat, Active Frog Stretch

**Ankle:** Extended Ankle CARs, Eccentric Calf Raises, Wall Dorsiflexion, Single-Leg Balance, Supported Deep Squat

**Core:** Dead Bugs, Pallof Press, Bird Dogs, Hollow Body Hold, Copenhagen Plank, Suitcase Carry, Bear Crawl

That is 28 exercises across 4 sessions. Currently each session has 7-9 exercises, which is too many for 15-18 minutes.

## PRIORITY RANKING BY GATE TEST RELEVANCE

Gate tests: pain-free planks, 8 strict pull-ups, 15 clean dips, full Cossack squat, 30s single-leg balance, 4in wall dorsiflexion, 30s hollow body hold, overhead wall test.

**Highest priority** (directly tested or prerequisite for a test):
- Hollow Body Hold (gate test)
- Cossack Squat (gate test)
- Single-Leg Balance (gate test)
- Wall Dorsiflexion (gate test)
- Dead Hangs (pull-up/dip prerequisite + overhead test)
- Scapular Push-ups (plank pain fix + dip prerequisite)
- Banded External Rotation (shoulder stability for pressing/overhead)
- Eccentric Calf Raises (Achilles health, dorsiflexion prerequisite)
- Wall Slides (overhead wall test)
- ATG Split Squat (knee health, Cossack prerequisite)

**High priority** (strong support for gate tests):
- T-Spine Extension (overhead wall test support)
- Banded TKE (knee stability for Cossack)
- Pallof Press (anti-rotation, plank support)
- Dead Bugs (anti-extension core, plank support)
- Copenhagen Plank (lateral stability for Cossack)
- Glute Bridge (hip activation for all lower body)

**Moderate priority** (good training but not directly gating):
- Prone Y-T-W Raises (shoulder strength)
- Bird Dogs (cross-body stabilization)
- 90/90 Hip Switches (hip mobility barometer)
- Banded Clamshells (glute med, Cossack support)
- Open Book (t-spine rotation)
- Suitcase Carry (anti-lateral flexion)
- Bear Crawl (integration)
- Active Frog Stretch (adductor mobility)
- Extended Ankle CARs (ankle ROM)
- Supported Deep Squat (integration)

**Lower priority** (nice to have):
- Shoulder Sleeper Stretch (PAILs/RAILs)
- Tibialis Raises (shin strength, good but not gating)

---

## RESTRUCTURED ROUTINES

I'm going with **4 sessions (A/B/C/D)** but cutting each to 5-6 exercises. Every exercise in the program still appears somewhere. The exercises that got cut from main sessions move to an Add-on list.

---

```
ROUTINE A: Shoulder Stability
Target time: 16 minutes

1. Dead Hangs
   Hevy sets: 3 | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~3 min with rest)

2. Scapular Push-ups
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 10
   (~2 min)

3. Banded External Rotation at 90°
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per arm) | Per-set: Reps 12
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral, set per side)

4. Wall Slides
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 10
   (~2 min)

5. T-Spine Extension Over Foam Roller
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 10 + 30s hold
   (~3 min)

6. Hollow Body Hold
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2.5 min with rest)

Total estimated: ~15 min
```

**Rationale:** Dead Hangs and Scapular Push-ups are the two highest-impact shoulder exercises (grip/lat decompression and serratus activation). External Rotation protects the rotator cuff. Wall Slides directly target the overhead wall test. T-Spine Extension unlocks the thoracic position that shoulders need. Hollow Body Hold is a gate test and trains the anti-extension that fixes plank shoulder pain. Prone Y-T-W gets cut to add-on (good exercise, but external rotation covers the rotator cuff priority). Open Book moved to Session D per prior restructure. Shoulder Sleeper Stretch is cut to add-on (PAILs/RAILs is great but costs 4+ min for both sides).

---

```
ROUTINE B: Knee & Hip
Target time: 17 minutes

1. Glute Bridge with 3-sec Hold
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 12
   (~2 min)

2. Banded TKE
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

3. ATG Split Squat (Regressed)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Reps 8
   (~3 min — sequential unilateral)

4. Cossack Squat (Partial ROM)
   Hevy sets: 1 | Per-set: Reps 12 (alternating, 6 per side)
   (~2 min — bilateral alternating, doubled rep count)

5. Active Frog Stretch
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Duration 45s
   (~2.5 min)

6. Pallof Press
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Reps 10
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

Total estimated: ~14.5 min
```

**Rationale:** Glute Bridge activates glutes before the loading exercises. Banded TKE isolates VMO for Cossack squat prep. ATG Split Squat is the best knee bulletproofing exercise. Cossack Squat is a gate test (1 set of 12 alternating = 6 per side, enough stimulus at this phase). Active Frog Stretch directly addresses adductor tightness limiting Cossack depth. Pallof Press trains the anti-rotation stability that supports Cossack squats. 90/90 Hip Switches and Banded Clamshells move to add-on. Tibialis Raises move to Session C per prior restructure. Copenhagen Plank moves to Session D per prior restructure.

---

```
ROUTINE C: Ankle/Achilles & Integration
Target time: 16 minutes

1. Extended Ankle CARs
   Hevy sets: 4 (2 per foot, 1 per direction) | Per-set: Reps 8
   (~3 min — sequential unilateral)

2. Eccentric Calf Raises (Alfredson Protocol)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~3 min — sequential unilateral, slow eccentrics)

3. Wall Dorsiflexion Mobilization
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2 min — sequential unilateral)

4. Single-Leg Balance (Eyes Closed)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2 min — sequential unilateral)

5. Tibialis Anterior Raises
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~2 min)

6. Supported Deep Squat Hold
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2 min)

Total estimated: ~14 min
```

**Rationale:** This session is purely ankle/Achilles and lower-leg focused. Extended Ankle CARs maps the range. Eccentric Calf Raises are the gold standard for Achilles tendon health. Wall Dorsiflexion is a gate test (4in). Single-Leg Balance is a gate test (30s). Tibialis Raises protect the knee from below (moved here from B per prior restructure). Supported Deep Squat integrates ankle, knee, and hip mobility into one position. Bear Crawl moves to add-on.

---

```
ROUTINE D: Core & T-Spine
Target time: 17 minutes

1. Dead Bugs
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 16 (alternating, 8 per side)
   (~2.5 min — bilateral alternating, doubled rep count)

2. Bird Dogs
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 16 (alternating, 8 per side)
   (~2.5 min — bilateral alternating, doubled rep count)

3. Copenhagen Plank
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Duration 20s
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

4. Suitcase Carry
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

5. Open Book (Thread the Needle)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Reps 8
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

6. Banded Clamshells
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

Total estimated: ~15 min
```

**Rationale:** Dead Bugs and Bird Dogs are the anti-extension and cross-body stabilization foundation. Copenhagen Plank trains lateral core AND adductors (moved here from B per prior restructure). Suitcase Carry is anti-lateral-flexion under load. Open Book adds the t-spine rotation component (moved here from A per prior restructure). Banded Clamshells land here since they pair well with Copenhagen Plank (both target glute med/adductors from different angles) and got displaced from Session B. This session covers all three core pillars: anti-extension (Dead Bugs), anti-rotation (Bird Dogs), and anti-lateral-flexion (Suitcase Carry, Copenhagen Plank).

---

## ADD-ON LIST (for days with extra time)

These exercises were cut from main sessions to meet the 15-18 min constraint. Add 1-2 when Ryan has an extra 5-10 minutes:

1. **Prone Y-T-W Raises** — Pair with Session A. Excellent shoulder strengthening but External Rotation covers the rotator cuff priority.
2. **Shoulder Sleeper Stretch with PAILs/RAILs** — Pair with Session A. High-value stretch but costs 4+ minutes for both sides. Do on rest days or after CARs.
3. **90/90 Hip Switches** — Pair with Session B. Great hip mobility barometer. Can replace the Active Frog Stretch slot on any given day for variety.
4. **Bear Crawl** — Pair with Session C or D. Good integration movement. 2 sets of 30s.
5. **Hollow Body Hold (extra set)** — Already in Session A at 2 sets. Add a 3rd set on any session day after the main routine.

---

## INTENSITY PROGRESSION (WITHOUT ADDING TIME)

The time budget stays at 15-18 minutes in all phases. Progression comes from difficulty within the same set/rep structure:

**Foundation (Weeks 1-2):**
- CARs at 70% effort
- All exercises at bodyweight or lightest band
- ATG Split Squat with bench support, partial depth
- Cossack Squat holding doorframe, partial depth
- Hollow Body with knees bent, arms at sides
- Copenhagen Plank from knees, 10-15s
- Dead Hangs: 20s target
- Eccentric Calf Raises: both-leg lower if needed

**Build (Weeks 3-4):**
- CARs at 85% effort, actively pushing end ranges
- Increase band resistance one level (External Rotation, TKE, Pallof)
- ATG Split Squat: reduce support, increase depth
- Cossack Squat: less support, aim for parallel thigh
- Hollow Body with legs extended
- Copenhagen Plank full version, 20s
- Dead Hangs progress to active hangs (shoulder depression)
- Eccentric Calf Raises: full single-leg protocol
- Wall Dorsiflexion: push foot further from wall

**Load (Weeks 5-8):**
- CARs at full effort with irradiation
- ATG Split Squat with light dumbbells, no support
- Cossack Squat goblet-loaded with light dumbbell
- Hollow Body rocks (same time, harder movement)
- Copenhagen Plank with hip dips
- Dead Hangs: add scapular pull-ups at top of set
- Suitcase Carry at 30-40 lb
- Dead Bugs with band resistance
- Y-T-W (if done as add-on): increase dumbbell 2-3 lb
- Single-Leg Balance on unstable surface (towel/pillow)
- Supported Deep Squat: goblet-loaded

Key principle: same number of sets, same rest periods, same total time. The exercises get harder through depth, load, surface instability, and movement complexity.

---

## GATE TEST COVERAGE CHECK

| Gate Test | Primary Exercise(s) | Session |
|---|---|---|
| Pain-free planks | Scapular Push-ups, Hollow Body Hold, Dead Bugs | A, D |
| 8 strict pull-ups | Dead Hangs (grip + lat), Scapular Push-ups (scap control) | A |
| 15 clean dips | Dead Hangs (shoulder mobility), External Rotation (cuff stability), Scapular Push-ups | A |
| Full Cossack squat | Cossack Squat, ATG Split Squat, Banded TKE, Active Frog Stretch, Copenhagen Plank, Banded Clamshells | B, D |
| 30s single-leg balance | Single-Leg Balance | C |
| 4in wall dorsiflexion | Wall Dorsiflexion, Eccentric Calf Raises, Extended Ankle CARs | C |
| 30s hollow body hold | Hollow Body Hold, Dead Bugs | A, D |
| Overhead wall test | Wall Slides, T-Spine Extension, Dead Hangs, Open Book | A, D |

All 8 gate tests are covered by exercises in the main sessions. No gaps.

---

## WEEKLY SCHEDULE COMPATIBILITY

Using the existing 4-day template as an example:
- Mon: CARs (10 min) + Session A (16 min) = 26 min
- Wed: CARs (10 min) + Session B (15 min) = 25 min
- Fri: CARs (10 min) + Session C (14 min) = 24 min
- One additional day: CARs (10 min) + Session D (15 min) = 25 min

All within the 25-30 minute total window. The 5-day and 6-day templates work the same way since every session is under 18 min.

toolUseResult.prompt:
You are an expert fitness coach. You previously restructured Ryan's routines, but the time constraint was misunderstood. Here's the correction:

## CORRECTED CONSTRAINT

Ryan's total workout window is **25-30 minutes, including Daily CARs**. Every session day includes CARs as a warmup, so:
- Daily CARs: ~10 min
- Main session (A, B, C, or D): **15-18 minutes max**
- Total: 25-28 minutes

**This time budget is PERMANENT** — it does not expand in Build or Load phases. Do not plan to "progressively restore volume" — the volume must fit in 15-18 minutes per session at all phases. Load phase adds intensity (weight, depth, hold times), not more sets.

## WHAT STAYS FROM THE PREVIOUS RESTRUCTURE

Keep all the bilateral handling rules:
- Alternating exercises: double reps per set (dead bugs 8→16, bird dogs 8→16, hip switches 8→16, Cossack squats 6→12)
- Sequential unilateral: separate set per side (clamshells, calf raises, balance, external rotation, etc.)
- CARs: reps per direction within set; separate set per limb
- Exercise redistributions (Open Book→D, Tibialis→C, Copenhagen Plank→D, Dead Bugs→D only)

## WHAT NEEDS TO CHANGE

With only 15-18 minutes for the main session, most sessions will need 5-6 exercises max (not 7-8). You need to make harder cuts.

**Options to consider:**
1. Remove exercises that are lower-priority for gate tests
2. Reduce to 1 set for some exercises (1 set of a bilateral exercise = 2 sets in Hevy counting both sides)
3. Combine sessions differently — maybe 3 sessions instead of 4, rotating exercises
4. Move some exercises to "add-on" status for days Ryan has extra time

## CONTEXT REMINDERS

- Ryan is 46, Achilles repair, shoulder instability, desk worker
- Phase 1 gate tests: pain-free planks, 8 strict pull-ups, 15 clean dips, full Cossack squat, 30s single-leg balance, 4in wall dorsiflexion, 30s hollow body hold, overhead wall test
- Long-term goals (flexible timeline): muscle-ups, tuck planche, pistol squats, handstands
- All exercises must still appear SOMEWHERE in the weekly plan (but not necessarily every session)

## Daily CARs (from previous restructure, keep as-is at ~10 min)

1. Neck CARs — 1 set × 3 each direction (6 total)
2. Shoulder CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per arm, 6 reps each = 12 total)
3. Hip CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per leg, 6 reps each = 12 total)
4. Ankle CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per foot, 6 reps each = 12 total)
5. Spine CARs — 1 set × 5
6. Wrist CARs — 2 sets × 3 each direction (set per wrist, 6 reps each = 12 total)
7. T-Spine Rotation — 2 sets × 5 (set per side)

## OUTPUT FORMAT

For each routine, provide:

```
ROUTINE: [name]
Target time: [X] minutes (must be ≤18 min)

1. Exercise Name
   Hevy sets: [N] | Per-set: Reps [X] or Duration [Xs]

2. ...
```

After routines, provide:
- A brief "Add-on" list: exercises that got cut from main sessions but should be done when Ryan has extra time
- How intensity progresses in Build and Load phases WITHOUT adding time
- Gate test coverage check

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the current program. Let me analyze the exercise inventory and time budgets carefully before designing the restructured routines.

Here is the full analysis and restructured plan:

---

## TIME BUDGET ANALYSIS

Each main session must fit in **15-18 minutes**. That means roughly:
- 5 exercises at ~3 min each = 15 min
- 6 exercises at ~2.5 min each = 15 min
- 6 exercises at ~3 min each = 18 min

So the hard ceiling is **6 exercises per session**, and some need to be quick (1-2 sets).

## EXERCISE INVENTORY (non-CARs, non-recovery)

From the current program, the main session exercises are:

**Shoulder:** Dead Hangs, T-Spine Extension (Foam Roller), Open Book, Scapular Push-ups, Banded External Rotation, Prone Y-T-W Raises, Wall Slides, Shoulder Sleeper Stretch

**Knee/Hip:** 90/90 Hip Switches, Glute Bridge, Banded Clamshells, Tibialis Raises, Banded TKE, ATG Split Squat, Cossack Squat, Active Frog Stretch

**Ankle:** Extended Ankle CARs, Eccentric Calf Raises, Wall Dorsiflexion, Single-Leg Balance, Supported Deep Squat

**Core:** Dead Bugs, Pallof Press, Bird Dogs, Hollow Body Hold, Copenhagen Plank, Suitcase Carry, Bear Crawl

That is 28 exercises across 4 sessions. Currently each session has 7-9 exercises, which is too many for 15-18 minutes.

## PRIORITY RANKING BY GATE TEST RELEVANCE

Gate tests: pain-free planks, 8 strict pull-ups, 15 clean dips, full Cossack squat, 30s single-leg balance, 4in wall dorsiflexion, 30s hollow body hold, overhead wall test.

**Highest priority** (directly tested or prerequisite for a test):
- Hollow Body Hold (gate test)
- Cossack Squat (gate test)
- Single-Leg Balance (gate test)
- Wall Dorsiflexion (gate test)
- Dead Hangs (pull-up/dip prerequisite + overhead test)
- Scapular Push-ups (plank pain fix + dip prerequisite)
- Banded External Rotation (shoulder stability for pressing/overhead)
- Eccentric Calf Raises (Achilles health, dorsiflexion prerequisite)
- Wall Slides (overhead wall test)
- ATG Split Squat (knee health, Cossack prerequisite)

**High priority** (strong support for gate tests):
- T-Spine Extension (overhead wall test support)
- Banded TKE (knee stability for Cossack)
- Pallof Press (anti-rotation, plank support)
- Dead Bugs (anti-extension core, plank support)
- Copenhagen Plank (lateral stability for Cossack)
- Glute Bridge (hip activation for all lower body)

**Moderate priority** (good training but not directly gating):
- Prone Y-T-W Raises (shoulder strength)
- Bird Dogs (cross-body stabilization)
- 90/90 Hip Switches (hip mobility barometer)
- Banded Clamshells (glute med, Cossack support)
- Open Book (t-spine rotation)
- Suitcase Carry (anti-lateral flexion)
- Bear Crawl (integration)
- Active Frog Stretch (adductor mobility)
- Extended Ankle CARs (ankle ROM)
- Supported Deep Squat (integration)

**Lower priority** (nice to have):
- Shoulder Sleeper Stretch (PAILs/RAILs)
- Tibialis Raises (shin strength, good but not gating)

---

## RESTRUCTURED ROUTINES

I'm going with **4 sessions (A/B/C/D)** but cutting each to 5-6 exercises. Every exercise in the program still appears somewhere. The exercises that got cut from main sessions move to an Add-on list.

---

```
ROUTINE A: Shoulder Stability
Target time: 16 minutes

1. Dead Hangs
   Hevy sets: 3 | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~3 min with rest)

2. Scapular Push-ups
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 10
   (~2 min)

3. Banded External Rotation at 90°
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per arm) | Per-set: Reps 12
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral, set per side)

4. Wall Slides
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 10
   (~2 min)

5. T-Spine Extension Over Foam Roller
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 10 + 30s hold
   (~3 min)

6. Hollow Body Hold
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2.5 min with rest)

Total estimated: ~15 min
```

**Rationale:** Dead Hangs and Scapular Push-ups are the two highest-impact shoulder exercises (grip/lat decompression and serratus activation). External Rotation protects the rotator cuff. Wall Slides directly target the overhead wall test. T-Spine Extension unlocks the thoracic position that shoulders need. Hollow Body Hold is a gate test and trains the anti-extension that fixes plank shoulder pain. Prone Y-T-W gets cut to add-on (good exercise, but external rotation covers the rotator cuff priority). Open Book moved to Session D per prior restructure. Shoulder Sleeper Stretch is cut to add-on (PAILs/RAILs is great but costs 4+ min for both sides).

---

```
ROUTINE B: Knee & Hip
Target time: 17 minutes

1. Glute Bridge with 3-sec Hold
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 12
   (~2 min)

2. Banded TKE
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

3. ATG Split Squat (Regressed)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Reps 8
   (~3 min — sequential unilateral)

4. Cossack Squat (Partial ROM)
   Hevy sets: 1 | Per-set: Reps 12 (alternating, 6 per side)
   (~2 min — bilateral alternating, doubled rep count)

5. Active Frog Stretch
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Duration 45s
   (~2.5 min)

6. Pallof Press
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Reps 10
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

Total estimated: ~14.5 min
```

**Rationale:** Glute Bridge activates glutes before the loading exercises. Banded TKE isolates VMO for Cossack squat prep. ATG Split Squat is the best knee bulletproofing exercise. Cossack Squat is a gate test (1 set of 12 alternating = 6 per side, enough stimulus at this phase). Active Frog Stretch directly addresses adductor tightness limiting Cossack depth. Pallof Press trains the anti-rotation stability that supports Cossack squats. 90/90 Hip Switches and Banded Clamshells move to add-on. Tibialis Raises move to Session C per prior restructure. Copenhagen Plank moves to Session D per prior restructure.

---

```
ROUTINE C: Ankle/Achilles & Integration
Target time: 16 minutes

1. Extended Ankle CARs
   Hevy sets: 4 (2 per foot, 1 per direction) | Per-set: Reps 8
   (~3 min — sequential unilateral)

2. Eccentric Calf Raises (Alfredson Protocol)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~3 min — sequential unilateral, slow eccentrics)

3. Wall Dorsiflexion Mobilization
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2 min — sequential unilateral)

4. Single-Leg Balance (Eyes Closed)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per leg) | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2 min — sequential unilateral)

5. Tibialis Anterior Raises
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~2 min)

6. Supported Deep Squat Hold
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2 min)

Total estimated: ~14 min
```

**Rationale:** This session is purely ankle/Achilles and lower-leg focused. Extended Ankle CARs maps the range. Eccentric Calf Raises are the gold standard for Achilles tendon health. Wall Dorsiflexion is a gate test (4in). Single-Leg Balance is a gate test (30s). Tibialis Raises protect the knee from below (moved here from B per prior restructure). Supported Deep Squat integrates ankle, knee, and hip mobility into one position. Bear Crawl moves to add-on.

---

```
ROUTINE D: Core & T-Spine
Target time: 17 minutes

1. Dead Bugs
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 16 (alternating, 8 per side)
   (~2.5 min — bilateral alternating, doubled rep count)

2. Bird Dogs
   Hevy sets: 2 | Per-set: Reps 16 (alternating, 8 per side)
   (~2.5 min — bilateral alternating, doubled rep count)

3. Copenhagen Plank
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Duration 20s
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

4. Suitcase Carry
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Duration 30s
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

5. Open Book (Thread the Needle)
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Reps 8
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

6. Banded Clamshells
   Hevy sets: 2 (1 per side) | Per-set: Reps 15
   (~2.5 min — sequential unilateral)

Total estimated: ~15 min
```

**Rationale:** Dead Bugs and Bird Dogs are the anti-extension and cross-body stabilization foundation. Copenhagen Plank trains lateral core AND adductors (moved here from B per prior restructure). Suitcase Carry is anti-lateral-flexion under load. Open Book adds the t-spine rotation component (moved here from A per prior restructure). Banded Clamshells land here since they pair well with Copenhagen Plank (both target glute med/adductors from different angles) and got displaced from Session B. This session covers all three core pillars: anti-extension (Dead Bugs), anti-rotation (Bird Dogs), and anti-lateral-flexion (Suitcase Carry, Copenhagen Plank).

---

## ADD-ON LIST (for days with extra time)

These exercises were cut from main sessions to meet the 15-18 min constraint. Add 1-2 when Ryan has an extra 5-10 minutes:

1. **Prone Y-T-W Raises** — Pair with Session A. Excellent shoulder strengthening but External Rotation covers the rotator cuff priority.
2. **Shoulder Sleeper Stretch with PAILs/RAILs** — Pair with Session A. High-value stretch but costs 4+ minutes for both sides. Do on rest days or after CARs.
3. **90/90 Hip Switches** — Pair with Session B. Great hip mobility barometer. Can replace the Active Frog Stretch slot on any given day for variety.
4. **Bear Crawl** — Pair with Session C or D. Good integration movement. 2 sets of 30s.
5. **Hollow Body Hold (extra set)** — Already in Session A at 2 sets. Add a 3rd set on any session day after the main routine.

---

## INTENSITY PROGRESSION (WITHOUT ADDING TIME)

The time budget stays at 15-18 minutes in all phases. Progression comes from difficulty within the same set/rep structure:

**Foundation (Weeks 1-2):**
- CARs at 70% effort
- All exercises at bodyweight or lightest band
- ATG Split Squat with bench support, partial depth
- Cossack Squat holding doorframe, partial depth
- Hollow Body with knees bent, arms at sides
- Copenhagen Plank from knees, 10-15s
- Dead Hangs: 20s target
- Eccentric Calf Raises: both-leg lower if needed

**Build (Weeks 3-4):**
- CARs at 85% effort, actively pushing end ranges
- Increase band resistance one level (External Rotation, TKE, Pallof)
- ATG Split Squat: reduce support, increase depth
- Cossack Squat: less support, aim for parallel thigh
- Hollow Body with legs extended
- Copenhagen Plank full version, 20s
- Dead Hangs progress to active hangs (shoulder depression)
- Eccentric Calf Raises: full single-leg protocol
- Wall Dorsiflexion: push foot further from wall

**Load (Weeks 5-8):**
- CARs at full effort with irradiation
- ATG Split Squat with light dumbbells, no support
- Cossack Squat goblet-loaded with light dumbbell
- Hollow Body rocks (same time, harder movement)
- Copenhagen Plank with hip dips
- Dead Hangs: add scapular pull-ups at top of set
- Suitcase Carry at 30-40 lb
- Dead Bugs with band resistance
- Y-T-W (if done as add-on): increase dumbbell 2-3 lb
- Single-Leg Balance on unstable surface (towel/pillow)
- Supported Deep Squat: goblet-loaded

Key principle: same number of sets, same rest periods, same total time. The exercises get harder through depth, load, surface instability, and movement complexity.

---

## GATE TEST COVERAGE CHECK

| Gate Test | Primary Exercise(s) | Session |
|---|---|---|
| Pain-free planks | Scapular Push-ups, Hollow Body Hold, Dead Bugs | A, D |
| 8 strict pull-ups | Dead Hangs (grip + lat), Scapular Push-ups (scap control) | A |
| 15 clean dips | Dead Hangs (shoulder mobility), External Rotation (cuff stability), Scapular Push-ups | A |
| Full Cossack squat | Cossack Squat, ATG Split Squat, Banded TKE, Active Frog Stretch, Copenhagen Plank, Banded Clamshells | B, D |
| 30s single-leg balance | Single-Leg Balance | C |
| 4in wall dorsiflexion | Wall Dorsiflexion, Eccentric Calf Raises, Extended Ankle CARs | C |
| 30s hollow body hold | Hollow Body Hold, Dead Bugs | A, D |
| Overhead wall test | Wall Slides, T-Spine Extension, Dead Hangs, Open Book | A, D |

All 8 gate tests are covered by exercises in the main sessions. No gaps.

---

## WEEKLY SCHEDULE COMPATIBILITY

Using the existing 4-day template as an example:
- Mon: CARs (10 min) + Session A (16 min) = 26 min
- Wed: CARs (10 min) + Session B (15 min) = 25 min
- Fri: CARs (10 min) + Session C (14 min) = 24 min
- One additional day: CARs (10 min) + Session D (15 min) = 25 min

All within the 25-30 minute total window. The 5-day and 6-day templates work the same way since every session is under 18 min.

Here's the revised plan fitting CARs + session into 25-30 minutes:

## Restructured Sessions (15-17 min each, + 10 min CARs = 25-27 min total)

### Session A: Shoulder Stability (16 min)
1. Dead Hangs — 3 sets × 30s
2. Scapular Push-ups — 2 sets × 10 reps
3. Banded External Rotation — 2 sets × 12 (1 per arm)
4. Wall Slides — 2 sets × 10
5. T-Spine Extension Foam Roller — 2 sets × 10 + 30s hold
6. Hollow Body Hold — 2 sets × 30s

### Session B: Knee & Hip (15 min)
1. Glute Bridge — 2 sets × 12
2. Banded TKE — 2 sets × 15 (1 per leg)
3. ATG Split Squat — 2 sets × 8 (1 per leg)
4. Cossack Squat — 1 set × 12 (alternating)
5. Active Frog Stretch — 2 sets × 45s
6. Pallof Press — 2 sets × 10 (1 per side)

### Session C: Ankle/Achilles (14 min)
1. Extended Ankle CARs — 4 sets × 8 (2 per foot, per direction)
2. Eccentric Calf Raises — 2 sets × 15 (1 per leg)
3. Wall Dorsiflexion — 2 sets × 30s (1 per side)
4. Single-Leg Balance — 2 sets × 30s (1 per leg)
5. Tibialis Raises — 2 sets × 15
6. Supported Deep Squat — 2 sets × 30s

### Session D: Core & T-Spine (15 min)
1. Dead Bugs — 2 sets × 16 (alternating)
2. Bird Dogs — 2 sets × 16 (alternating)
3. Copenhagen Plank — 2 sets × 20s (1 per side)
4. Suitcase Carry — 2 sets × 30s (1 per side)
5. Open Book — 2 sets × 8 (1 per side)
6. Banded Clamshells — 2 sets × 15 (1 per side)

### Add-ons (when you have extra time)
- Prone Y-T-W Raises, Shoulder Sleeper Stretch, 90/90 Hip Switches, Bear Crawl

**Progression is intensity-only** (depth, load, band resistance, surface difficulty) — same sets/time throughout all phases. All 8 gate tests covered.

exercises.notes: The king of core exercises for your situation. Lie on back, arms up, knees at 90°. Press lower back into floor — this is the cue that matters. Extend opposite arm and leg while maintaining ZERO low back movement. Slow and controlled — 3 seconds out, 3 seconds back. If your back arches, shorten the range.

Scale it: bent knees with arms at sides (easiest) → straight legs with arms at sides → straight legs with arms overhead (hardest). If you can hold 30s at the hardest version, you can progress to hollow body rocks.

  "progressions": [
    {
      "id": "foundation",
      "weekRange": "Weeks 1-2",
      "phaseName": "Foundation",
      "focus": "Learn the movements. Don't chase depth or load.",
      "weekStart": 1,
      "weekEnd": 2,
      "sortOrder": 1,
      "details": [
        "CARs: 70% effort — find your current range, don't push past it",
        "All exercises at bodyweight or lightest band/dumbbell",
        "ATG Split Squat: use bench support, partial depth only",
        "Cossack Squat: hold doorframe, go only as deep as comfortable",
        "Eccentric calf raises: both-leg lower if single-leg is too intense on the right",
        "Dead hangs: aim for 15-20s, build to 30s",
        "Core: Dead bugs with bent knees, hollow body with knees bent and arms at sides, Copenhagen plank from knees (10-15s)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "build",
      "weekRange": "Weeks 3-4",
      "phaseName": "Build",
      "focus": "Add range and time under tension. Reduce assistance.",
      "weekStart": 3,
      "weekEnd": 4,
      "sortOrder": 2,
      "details": [
        "CARs: 85% effort — actively push into end ranges",
        "Increase band resistance one level on external rotations and TKEs",
        "ATG Split Squat: reduce bench support, increase depth",
        "Cossack Squat: less support, aim for parallel thigh depth",
        "Eccentric calf raises: full single-leg protocol",
        "Dead hangs: progress to active hangs (depress shoulders while hanging)",
        "Add 5-10s to all timed holds",
        "Core: Dead bugs with straight legs, hollow body with legs extended, Copenhagen plank full (20-30s), add Pallof press with medium band"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "load",
      "weekRange": "Weeks 5-8",
      "phaseName": "Load",
      "focus": "Start adding external load to mobility positions.",
      "weekStart": 5,
      "weekEnd": 8,
      "sortOrder": 3,
      "details": [
        "CARs: full effort with irradiation (squeeze fist, brace core)",
        "Goblet Cossack Squats with light dumbbell",
        "ATG Split Squat with light dumbbells, no support",
        "Weighted deep squat holds (goblet position)",
        "Y-T-W raises: increase dumbbell weight by 2-3 lb",
        "Begin testing overhead press and front squat with improved positions",
        "Single-leg balance on unstable surface (folded towel or pillow)",
        "Core: Hollow body rocks, Copenhagen plank with hip dips, suitcase carries at 30-40 lb, dead bugs with band resistance"
      ]
    }
  ],

  "benchmarks": [
    {
      "id": "wall-dorsiflexion",
      "name": "Wall Dorsiflexion Test",
      "howTo": "Face wall, foot back from wall. Drive knee forward keeping heel down. Measure max distance (inches) where knee touches wall and heel stays down.",
      "target": "Left: 4-5 inches. Right (Achilles): will start lower — track improvement, don't compare sides.",
      "frequency": "Test day 1, then every 2 weeks",
      "unit": "inches"
    },
    {
      "id": "single-leg-balance-test",
      "name": "Single-Leg Balance (Eyes Closed)",
      "howTo": "Stand on one foot, close eyes, time how long before you touch down or open eyes.",

lines: "notes": "Hold doorframe, go only as deep as comfortable."
"notes": "Less support, aim for parallel thigh depth."

"notes": "The king of core exercises for your situation. Lie on back, arms up, knees at 90°. Press lower back into floor — this is the cue that matters. Extend opposite arm and leg while maintaining ZERO low back movement. Slow and controlled — 3 seconds out, 3 seconds back. If your back arches, shorten the range. Progress by holding a light dumbbell overhead or adding a band around your feet."

"notes": "Arms overhead, legs extended, low back pressed into floor, shoulders and feet lifted. Scale it: bent knees with arms at sides (easiest) → straight legs with arms at sides → straight legs with arms overhead (hardest). If you can hold 30s at the hardest version, you can progress to hollow body rocks."

"notes": "Side plank with top leg on bench, bottom leg free. Trains lateral core and adductors simultaneously. Regression: bend bottom knee to floor, or use a shorter bench so you're supporting from the knee instead of the foot. Progression: add hip dips (lower and lift hips while holding). This exercise alone can fix a lot of your lateral movement issues."

content.text: Let me verify the Hevy updates took effect and answer your question about the database.

Hevy routines are confirmed updated (you can see Daily CARs now has the correct sets in Hevy). Here's the status:

**Updated:**
- All 6 Hevy routines via API (what you see when you start a workout)
- Program JSON file on disk (for future activations)

**Not updated:**
- The D1 database. The program was stored as a JSON blob in D1 when you activated it. The companion app reads from D1, so it would still show old prescription text (e.g., "3×15 each leg"). The actual Hevy routine is correct, but the companion app's display would be stale.

To fix the D1 data, we'd need to either:
1. Update the `json_data` column in the `programs` table directly via wrangler
2. Re-activate the program (but that would recreate queue items, losing your completion history)

Option 1 is safe — it just updates the display data without touching queue items or mappings. Want me to set that up?

content.content:

12

[env.production]

database_name = "hevy-planner"
database_id = "856703c5-ee18-43c5-b910-4ee8f27742f4"
[env.production.vars]
ENVIRONMENT = "production"

Base directory for this skill: /Users/ryanr/.claude/skills/retrospective

# Retrospective

Review the current session to extract durable improvements. Proposes changes — never writes without approval.

## Process

### 1. Mine the session

Scan the conversation for:

- **Gotchas hit** — compiler errors, API surprises, type system quirks, wrong assumptions
- **Patterns discovered** — code patterns, test patterns, workflow patterns that worked well
- **Workarounds used** — things that should have been obvious but weren't
- **Repeated manual steps** — anything done 2+ times that could be automated
- **Review findings** — issues caught by code review that indicate missing rules
- **Corrections received** — user said "no", "don't", "stop", "actually" — these are gold

### 2. Categorize findings

Sort each finding into exactly one bucket:

| Bucket | Goes where | Example |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| **Rule/gotcha** | CLAUDE.md | "F# task CEs need type annotations on let! bindings" |
| **Repeated workflow** | New skill | "Every PR needs expert review then fix cycle" |
| **Repeated agent dispatch** | New agent | "Always dispatch F# code reviewer with same prompt" |
| **Project-specific fact** | Memory file | "Frank.Tests is not in Frank.sln" |
| **Already covered** | Nowhere | Rule already in CLAUDE.md or constitution |
| **One-off** | Nowhere | Unique to this task, won't recur |

### 3. Propose changes

Present findings as a numbered list, grouped by bucket. For each:

```
[CLAUDE.md] Add to F# Patterns: "Wrap handler lambdas in RequestDelegate(...)
to resolve ResourceBuilder.Get overload ambiguity"
Reason: Hit this in JsonHomeProjection tests, cost 5 minutes debugging.
```

```
[SKILL] Create /expert-review skill — dispatched code-reviewer subagent with
5-reviewer prompt template 3 times this week. Same structure each time.
```

```
[SKIP] MemoryStream.ToArray() vs GetBuffer() — already added to CLAUDE.md
this session.
```

### 4. Wait for approval

**Never write changes without explicit approval.** Present the full list, let the user:
- Approve all
- Approve selectively ("do 1, 3, 5")
- Modify ("change #2 to say X instead")
- Reject ("skip all, nothing worth keeping")

### 5. Apply approved changes

- **CLAUDE.md updates**: Edit the file directly, adding to the appropriate section
- **New skills**: Create skill stub in `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` with frontmatter and skeleton. Note: full skill creation should follow superpowers:writing-skills TDD process — the stub is a starting point, not a finished skill.
- **New agents**: Create agent stub in `.claude/agents/<name>.md` with frontmatter
- **Memory updates**: Write to the project's memory directory

## What makes a good CLAUDE.md rule

- **Actionable**: "Use `RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> ...)` to resolve overload" not "Be careful with overloads"
- **Specific**: Include the exact pattern, not a vague principle
- **Born from pain**: Every rule should trace back to a real mistake or wasted time
- **Not duplicating the constitution**: Constitution covers principles; CLAUDE.md covers practical gotchas

## What makes a good skill candidate

A workflow is a skill candidate when:
- Done **2+ times** across sessions with the **same structure**
- Involves **judgment calls** (not just mechanical steps — those are hooks)
- Would benefit from a **checklist or decision tree**
- Is **portable** across projects (not Frank-specific)

## What makes a good agent candidate

A dispatch pattern is an agent candidate when:
- The **same prompt template** is used repeatedly
- It targets a **specific expertise** (F# reviewer, spec compliance, perf analysis)
- The agent needs **specific tools or permissions**
- Configuring it fresh each time wastes context

## When NOT to create anything

- **One-off solutions** — unique to this task, won't recur
- **Things the user already knows** — don't patronize with obvious rules
- **Premature abstractions** — wait for the third occurrence before creating a skill
- **Constitution-level principles** — those go through the formal amendment process

## Session Retrospective

### Findings

**1. [CLAUDE.md] Hevy API: `repRange` rejects null values inside the object**
```
Add to Common Mistakes to Avoid: "Hevy API rejects `repRange: {start: null, end: null}` 
— use `{start: 0, end: 0}` instead. The schema says nullable but the API disagrees."
```
Reason: First 3 API calls failed with `"rep_range" must be of type object`. Cost a retry round.

**2. [CLAUDE.md] Hevy API: `update-routine` replaces the full exercise list**
```
Add to Common Mistakes to Avoid: "Hevy `update-routine` replaces ALL exercises — 
always send the complete exercise list, not just changed exercises. Omitted exercises 
are deleted from the routine."
```
Reason: Test call with only 2 exercises wiped the other 5 from Daily CARs. Had to immediately resend the full list.

**3. [CLAUDE.md] Wrangler: `--file` uses the D1 import API which may fail with OAuth tokens**
```
Add to Common Mistakes to Avoid: "`wrangler d1 execute --file` uses the /import API 
endpoint which can fail with OAuth token auth (error 10000). Use `--command` for small 
queries or `d1 migrations apply` for larger SQL."
```
Reason: Had to pivot from `--file` to a migration approach after auth error.

**4. [MEMORY] Data migrations in schema migration directory are inappropriate**
```
Don't create data-only migrations (UPDATE statements) in the migrations/ directory — 
those are for schema changes. For one-off data fixes, use `wrangler d1 execute --command` 
or apply directly and don't commit the SQL.
```
Reason: User caught this and had me remove `0010_update_program_sets.sql` before push.

**5. [MEMORY] Program JSON sets field uses `×` (multiplication sign), not `x`**
```
When doing replace_all on program JSON set counts, the pattern is `"sets": "3×` 
(with Unicode ×), which safely targets only routine exercise prescriptions and 
progressionByPhase entries — not benchmark targets or other text that may contain 
similar patterns.
```
Reason: Had to carefully verify that replace_all wouldn't corrupt benchmark entries like `"3×8 full range on bar"`.

**6. [SKIP] Bilateral exercise handling rules — one-off domain knowledge, not a code pattern**

**7. [SKIP] Fitness coach agent dispatching — one-off, not a recurring pattern (yet)**

### Proposed Actions

| # | Bucket | Action |
|---|--------|--------|
| 1 | CLAUDE.md | Add Hevy `repRange` gotcha |
| 2 | CLAUDE.md | Add Hevy `update-routine` replaces-all warning |
| 3 | CLAUDE.md | Add wrangler `--file` auth gotcha |
| 4 | CLAUDE.md | Add "no data migrations in migrations/" rule |
| 5 | SKIP | Too narrow, unlikely to recur with same pattern |
| 6 | SKIP | One-off |
| 7 | SKIP | One-off |

Which would you like me to apply?

file.content:
```
hevy-planner/
├── CLAUDE.md
├── SPEC.md
├── schema/
│   └── program.schema.json     ← JSON Schema for program definition
├── .claude/
│   ├── skills/
│   └── agents/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                ← Worker entry point, routing
│   ├── domain/                 ← Queue engine, reflow, phase logic (pure functions)
│   ├── hevy/                   ← Hevy API client
│   ├── fragments/              ← Datastar HTML fragment builders
│   ├── storage/                ← D1 queries
│   ├── utils/                  ← Shared helpers (escapeHtml, escapeAttr, truncate)
│   └── auth/                   ← API key management
├── programs/
│   └── mobility-joint-restoration.json  ← first bundled program
├── migrations/                 ← D1 schema migrations
├── wrangler.toml
└── package.json
```

## Style & Conventions

- Domain functions are pure: data in, data out. No side effects.
- Datastar fragments return HTML strings. No JSX, no templating engine.
- D1 queries use prepared statements (no raw string interpolation).
- Program data lives in D1 — load per-request via `loadProgram(db, userId)`. No static imports for active program state.

content.content:
80	- Do NOT store Hevy API keys in plaintext — encrypt at rest in D1
81	- Do NOT poll Hevy on every page load — cache recent workout data, refresh on user action
82	- Do NOT define escapeHtml/escapeAttr/truncate locally in fragments — import from `src/utils/html.ts`
83	- Datastar SSE events must end with two newlines (`\n\n`)
84	- D1 has a 1MB response size limit per query — paginate workout history
85	- Hevy API rate limits are undocumented — add backoff/retry logic
86	- Do NOT assume Hevy API request/response shapes — verify against the live API (via MCP tools or curl) before writing client methods. Known quirks: POST `/exercise_templates` returns a plain string ID (not JSON), POST `/routines` returns `{ routine: [...] }` (array inside object), `folder_id` is required on routine creation, and field names differ from GET responses (e.g., `muscle_group` not `primary_muscle_group`). GET `/workouts` uses `title` (not `name`) for workout name.
87	- When building Maps from queue items for matching, use first-match-wins (`if (!map.has(key))`) so the front of the queue is matched, not the end. Multiple queue items share the same `hevy_routine_id`.
88	- When marking completions from Hevy, use the workout's `start_time` converted to user timezone (`request.cf.timezone`) — not `todayString()`. Users sync after midnight UTC but before local midnight, or sync the next day.
89	- Before matching workouts to queue items in sync, filter out workouts whose ID already appears as `hevy_workout_id` on a completed queue item. Otherwise each sync re-matches the same workout to a new pending item.
90	
91	## Current Phase
92	
93	**v1 deployed** — Core app on `main`, deployed to Cloudflare Workers production. Setup flow, Hevy sync (push + pull), queue with reflow, Daily CARs tracking, all pages rendering via Datastar SSE. 47 tests passing. All dates use user-local timezone via `request.cf.timezone`.
94

file.content:
- Do NOT store Hevy API keys in plaintext — encrypt at rest in D1
- Do NOT poll Hevy on every page load — cache recent workout data, refresh on user action
- Do NOT define escapeHtml/escapeAttr/truncate locally in fragments — import from `src/utils/html.ts`
- Datastar SSE events must end with two newlines (`\n\n`)
- D1 has a 1MB response size limit per query — paginate workout history
- Hevy API rate limits are undocumented — add backoff/retry logic
- Do NOT assume Hevy API request/response shapes — verify against the live API (via MCP tools or curl) before writing client methods. Known quirks: POST `/exercise_templates` returns a plain string ID (not JSON), POST `/routines` returns `{ routine: [...] }` (array inside object), `folder_id` is required on routine creation, and field names differ from GET responses (e.g., `muscle_group` not `primary_muscle_group`). GET `/workouts` uses `title` (not `name`) for workout name.
- When building Maps from queue items for matching, use first-match-wins (`if (!map.has(key))`) so the front of the queue is matched, not the end. Multiple queue items share the same `hevy_routine_id`.
- When marking completions from Hevy, use the workout's `start_time` converted to user timezone (`request.cf.timezone`) — not `todayString()`. Users sync after midnight UTC but before local midnight, or sync the next day.
- Before matching workouts to queue items in sync, filter out workouts whose ID already appears as `hevy_workout_id` on a completed queue item. Otherwise each sync re-matches the same workout to a new pending item.

## Current Phase

**v1 deployed** — Core app on `main`, deployed to Cloudflare Workers production. Setup flow, Hevy sync (push + pull), queue with reflow, Daily CARs tracking, all pages rendering via Datastar SSE. 47 tests passing. All dates use user-local timezone via `request.cf.timezone`.

## Current Phase
input.new_string:
- Before matching workouts to queue items in sync, filter out workouts whose ID already appears as `hevy_workout_id` on a completed queue item. Otherwise each sync re-matches the same workout to a new pending item.
- Hevy API rejects `repRange: {start: null, end: null}` — use `{start: 0, end: 0}` instead. The schema says nullable but the API disagrees.
- Hevy `update-routine` replaces ALL exercises — always send the complete exercise list, not just changed exercises. Omitted exercises are deleted from the routine.
- `wrangler d1 execute --file` uses the /import API endpoint which can fail with OAuth token auth (error 10000). Use `--command` for small queries or `d1 migrations apply` for larger SQL.
- The `migrations/` directory is for schema migrations only — do NOT commit data-only migrations (UPDATE statements). For one-off data fixes, use `wrangler d1 execute --command` directly.

## Current Phase

toolUseResult.newString:
- Before matching workouts to queue items in sync, filter out workouts whose ID already appears as `hevy_workout_id` on a completed queue item. Otherwise each sync re-matches the same workout to a new pending item.
- Hevy API rejects `repRange: {start: null, end: null}` — use `{start: 0, end: 0}` instead. The schema says nullable but the API disagrees.
- Hevy `update-routine` replaces ALL exercises — always send the complete exercise list, not just changed exercises. Omitted exercises are deleted from the routine.
- `wrangler d1 execute --file` uses the /import API endpoint which can fail with OAuth token auth (error 10000). Use `--command` for small queries or `d1 migrations apply` for larger SQL.
- The `migrations/` directory is for schema migrations only — do NOT commit data-only migrations (UPDATE statements). For one-off data fixes, use `wrangler d1 execute --command` directly.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

## Project Overview

A training companion web app that adds scheduling intelligence to Hevy. Read `SPEC.md` for the full product spec — screens, user flows, Hevy integration, and queue/reflow rules.

Hevy handles workout logging (sets, reps, PRs, Apple Watch). This companion handles everything else: program management, queue-based scheduling with automatic reflow, multi-phase roadmaps, benchmark tracking, and skill progression.

## Tech Stack

- **Runtime**: Cloudflare Workers (TypeScript)
- **Frontend**: Datastar (SSE-driven hypermedia, ~12KB, no build step)
- **Database**: Cloudflare D1 (SQLite at the edge)
- **Integration**: Hevy API (routines, workouts, exercises)
- **Deployment**: Cloudflare Workers (wrangler)
- **PWA**: Service worker + web app manifest for home screen install

## Architecture

The server owns the UI. Cloudflare Workers serves HTML pages with Datastar attributes. User interactions trigger SSE responses that push HTML fragment updates. No client-side framework, no build step, no SPA routing.

```
Workers (TypeScript)
├── Routes (request handling, content serving)
├── Domain (queue engine, reflow, phase logic — pure functions)
├── Datastar (SSE fragment builders, signal management)
├── Hevy (API client — routines, workouts, exercises)
└── Storage (D1 queries, user state, queue, benchmarks)
```

## Data Model

Programs use Hevy-compatible **Exercise Templates** + **Routines** (not "sessions"):

- **`exerciseTemplates`** — reusable exercise definitions with Hevy fields (type, equipmentCategory, primaryMuscleGroup) + coaching fields (notes, videoURL, tags, progressionByPhase). Defined once, referenced by routines.
- **`routines`** — ordered groups of exercise references. Each exercise entry has `exerciseTemplateId` + `sets` (the prescription for this routine context), with optional `notes` override.
- **`weekTemplates`** — days reference `routineIDs` (not sessionIDs).

D1 tables: `exercise_template_mappings` (our template ID → Hevy template ID), `routine_mappings` (our routine ID → Hevy routine ID), `queue_items` (uses `routine_id`).

## Project Structure

```
hevy-planner/
├── CLAUDE.md
├── SPEC.md
├── schema/
│   └── program.schema.json     ← JSON Schema for program definition
├── .claude/
│   ├── skills/
│   └── agents/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                ← Worker entry point, routing
│   ├── domain/                 ← Queue engine, reflow, phase logic (pure functions)
│   ├── hevy/                   ← Hevy API client
│   ├── fragments/              ← Datastar HTML fragment builders
│   ├── storage/                ← D1 queries
│   ├── utils/                  ← Shared helpers (escapeHtml, escapeAttr, truncate)
│   └── auth/                   ← API key management
├── programs/
│   └── mobility-joint-restoration.json  ← first bundled program
├── migrations/                 ← D1 schema migrations
├── wrangler.toml
└── package.json
```

## Style & Conventions

- Domain functions are pure: data in, data out. No side effects.
- Datastar fragments return HTML strings. No JSX, no templating engine.
- D1 queries use prepared statements (no raw string interpolation).
- Program data lives in D1 — load per-request via `loadProgram(db, userId)`. No static imports for active program state.
- `HevyEnumValues` uses camelCase field names (domain side). Route handlers translate to snake_case at Hevy API sync time: `exerciseType` → `exercise_type`, `equipmentCategory` → `equipment_category`, `primaryMuscleGroup` → `muscle_group` (Hevy uses `muscle_group`, not `primary_muscle_group`), `secondaryMuscleGroups` → `secondary_muscle_groups`.
- Dark theme: #141210 background, #e8e4df text.
- Commit messages: imperative mood, concise.

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- Do NOT put queue/reflow logic in route handlers — it belongs in domain/
- Do NOT store Hevy API keys in plaintext — encrypt at rest in D1
- Do NOT poll Hevy on every page load — cache recent workout data, refresh on user action
- Do NOT define escapeHtml/escapeAttr/truncate locally in fragments — import from `src/utils/html.ts`
- Datastar SSE events must end with two newlines (`\n\n`)
- D1 has a 1MB response size limit per query — paginate workout history
- Hevy API rate limits are undocumented — add backoff/retry logic
- Do NOT assume Hevy API request/response shapes — verify against the live API (via MCP tools or curl) before writing client methods. Known quirks: POST `/exercise_templates` returns a plain string ID (not JSON), POST `/routines` returns `{ routine: [...] }` (array inside object), `folder_id` is required on routine creation, and field names differ from GET responses (e.g., `muscle_group` not `primary_muscle_group`). GET `/workouts` uses `title` (not `name`) for workout name.
- When building Maps from queue items for matching, use first-match-wins (`if (!map.has(key))`) so the front of the queue is matched, not the end. Multiple queue items share the same `hevy_routine_id`.
- When marking completions from Hevy, use the workout's `start_time` converted to user timezone (`request.cf.timezone`) — not `todayString()`. Users sync after midnight UTC but before local midnight, or sync the next day.
- Before matching workouts to queue items in sync, filter out workouts whose ID already appears as `hevy_workout_id` on a completed queue item. Otherwise each sync re-matches the same workout to a new pending item.

## Current Phase

**v1 deployed** — Core app on `main`, deployed to Cloudflare Workers production. Setup flow, Hevy sync (push + pull), queue with reflow, Daily CARs tracking, all pages rendering via Datastar SSE. 47 tests passing. All dates use user-local timezone via `request.cf.timezone`.

- When marking completions from Hevy, use the workout's `start_time` converted to user timezone (`request.cf.timezone`) — not `todayString()`. Users sync after midnight UTC but before local midnight, or sync the next day.

- Before matching workouts to queue items in sync, filter out workouts whose ID already appears as `hevy_workout_id` on a completed queue item. Otherwise each sync re-matches the same workout to a new pending item.
+- Hevy API rejects `repRange: {start: null, end: null}` — use `{start: 0, end: 0}` instead. The schema says nullable but the API disagrees.
+- Hevy `update-routine` replaces ALL exercises — always send the complete exercise list, not just changed exercises. Omitted exercises are deleted from the routine.
+- `wrangler d1 execute --file` uses the /import API endpoint which can fail with OAuth token auth (error 10000). Use `--command` for small queries or `d1 migrations apply` for larger SQL.
+- The `migrations/` directory is for schema migrations only — do NOT commit data-only migrations (UPDATE statements). For one-off data fixes, use `wrangler d1 execute --command` directly.

Add Hevy API and wrangler gotchas to CLAUDE.md

Lessons from bilateral rep fix session: repRange rejects nulls,
update-routine replaces all exercises, wrangler --file auth issues,
and migrations/ is for schema only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
